key_insight: Catalyst discovery for ammonia synthesis can be framed as a multi-intermediate reaction-network problem, and only ten-million-scale generative exploration revealed surfaces compatible across all four key intermediates.
engineering_relevance: This approach provides a scalable screening strategy that can recover known Fe/Ru motifs while surfacing new catalyst families for dissociative and associative ammonia synthesis pathways.
method: Adsorbate-specific generative Transformers explored ~15 million configurations per intermediate (N*, NH*, NNH*, HNNH*).
Structural compression reduced the candidate set before high-throughput machine-learning-potential screening.
Representative DFT calculations validated pathway-specific leads such as Fe-V and Al-Pd-Zr.
evidence_signal: Scale-dependent overlap analysis showed the full four-intermediate compatibility space was missed at 10^5–10^6 scale and appeared only at ten-million-scale exploration, yielding 279 high-potential target materials.
watch_item: Because the workflow relies on ML potentials and compressed generated structures, transferability to experimentally synthesizable surfaces and adsorbate conditions still needs careful validation.
paper_title: Reaction-Network-Level Discovery of Ammonia Synthesis Catalysts via Ten-Million-Scale Generative Exploration
